items.meta: Quick reference for converting between Nm, kgf·m, ft·lb, and in·lb.
items.searchText: torque unit conversion quick reference for converting between nm, kgf·m, ft·lb, and in·lb. torque conversion chart nm kgf·m ft·lb in·lb 1 0.10 0.74 8.85 5 0.51 3.69 44.3 10 1.02 7.38 88.5 20 2.04 14.8 177 30 3.06 22.1 266 50 5.10 36.9 443 100 10.2 73.8 885 formula: nm × 0.7376 = ft·lb | nm × 8.851 = in·lb | nm × 0.10197 = kgf·m

items.title: Motorcycle Oil Grades Explained
items.meta: Understanding SAE viscosity ratings, JASO MA/MA2, and API classifications.
items.searchText: motorcycle oil grades explained understanding sae viscosity ratings, jaso ma/ma2, and api classifications. sae viscosity the first number (e.g. 10 w-40) indicates cold-start viscosity. the w means winter. lower numbers flow better in cold conditions. the second number (e.g. 10w- 40 ) indicates viscosity at operating temperature. higher numbers provide thicker protection at high heat. jaso certification jaso ma — standard for wet-clutch motorcycles. no friction modifiers that cause clutch slip. jaso ma2 — higher friction performance for modern bikes. preferred for most current motorcycles. jaso mb — for scooters with cvt. not suitable for manual-clutch bikes. api rating look for api sg or higher. avoid automotive oils with energy-conserving labels — they contain friction modifiers harmful to wet clutches.

items.title: Contributing to MotoX Wiki
items.meta: How to add or update motorcycle specifications.
items.searchText: contributing to motox wiki how to add or update motorcycle specifications. how to contribute motox wiki is community-maintained. you can contribute by: forking the repository on github editing the json files in data/ (motorcycles, pages, settings) committing changes and opening a pull request data guidelines always cite the source (service manual, oem documentation) include year ranges when specs vary by model year use standard units (nm for torque, l for volume) with conversions in parentheses mark unverified data in the notes field
